tiktok.caption: Your bank raised your credit limit 'as a reward' — here's what they're actually doing to you

youtube_short.title: That Credit Limit Increase Is a $2,400 Trap
title_alternatives: Why Your Bank Keeps Raising Your Credit Limit
youtube_short.description: Your bank raises your credit limit and calls it a reward — but 68% of people spend up to the new limit within 18 months. That's an average of $2,400 in new debt, plus $576 a year in interest at 24% APR. Banks analyze your spending history and time these offers to hit when you're most likely to swipe. Your counter-move? Call them and ask to lower it. Remove the ceiling, remove the trap. #creditcard #personalfinance #moneypsychology #debtfree #finance
